question.text: Under SEBI SAST Regulations 2011, "frequently traded shares" are defined as shares with a traded turnover of at least ___% of total shares in the preceding 12 calendar months.

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SEBI SAST Regulations 2011, frequently traded shares are those with a traded turnover of at least 10% of the total number of shares in the preceding 12 calendar months. This classification affects the pricing methodology for open offers. **The Trap:** 5% and 15% are commonly confused thresholds from other SEBI regulations. The 10% threshold is specific to the "frequently traded" definition under SAST. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) also uses liquidity thresholds for inclusion criteria, though the specific percentages differ. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Whether shares are "frequently traded" directly impacts the open offer price calculation — infrequently traded shares require a different (usually higher) valuation approach. Always verify trading volumes before pricing.
